article_meta.title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m

reasoning: This article is explicitly labeled as an 'open letter' written by an Iranian person ('Omid') speaking from Tehran, published by the New York Post. The entire article is essentially an attributed first-person account from a named source. The model systematically flags the source's own words and perspectives as bias, when nearly all of the flagged text belongs to the letter-writer Omid, not to the newspaper's editorial staff or journalist prose. The author prose is essentially limited to the headline, byline, and the final editorial note ('Omid spoke to the Post from Tehran via friends in California. Efforts have been made to protect his identity.'). The model also uses bias type labels not from the approved list (e.g., 'Pro-Trump Bias', 'Anti-Iran Regime Bias', 'Anti-Obama Bias', 'Anti-Left Media Bias', 'Pro-Reza Pahlavi Bias', 'Demonization', 'Selective Omission').

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Nearly all flagged text comes from Omid's first-person letter, which is an attributed source statement. The newspaper is publishing his open letter - his words are his, not the author/editor's prose. This is a systematic attribution rule violation, as the model treats the letter-writer's perspective as the newspaper's editorial bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All 7 flagged items are from the letter-writer's own expressed views, not from the newspaper's editorial prose. These are essentially all false positives for author-level bias detection since the newspaper is merely publishing an attributed letter.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: There is a legitimate case for flagging the newspaper's editorial choices - particularly 'Bias by Story Choice and Placement' for choosing to publish this one-sided open letter without counterbalancing perspectives, and possibly the headline framing. The model missed these actual author-level structural choices while flagging the source's own words.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All seven bias type labels used ('Pro-Trump Bias', 'Anti-Iran Regime Bias', 'Selective Omission', 'Anti-Obama Bias', 'Anti-Left Media Bias', 'Pro-Reza Pahlavi Bias', 'Demonization') are not from the approved list of valid bias types. This is a complete failure in type labeling.
